title: Intense Malaysian Fish Grill
description: A spicy, velvety grilled fish dish infused with a bold Malaysian spice paste and a creamy coconut sauce, dairy‑free and packed with adventure.
instructions: Preheat your grill to medium‑high heat, about 180°C.
In a bowl, whisk together chili flakes, lemongrass powder, onion powder, garlic powder, ginger, turmeric, tamarind paste, palm sugar, fish sauce, and coconut milk until sugar dissolves.
Pat the snapper fillets dry with paper towels and season lightly with salt.
Brush each fillet with vegetable oil to prevent sticking.
Place the fillets on the grill and cook for 4 to 5 minutes on each side, or until the fish flakes easily and develops a charred crust.
While the fish is grilling, pour the remaining sauce into a small saucepan and heat until it slightly thickens, about 2 to 3 minutes.
Remove the fillets from the grill and drizzle the thickened sauce over each piece.
Serve immediately with a side of jasmine rice or fresh greens.
